title: Error Handling and Recovery
description: Run errors are caught, classified, and delegated to registered error handlers. Currently, only MaxTurnsExceeded supports custom recovery; all other exceptions propagate to the caller.

use_cases.name: Register max_turns error handler
use_cases.description: Developer passes error_handlers={'max_turns': fn} to Runner.run/run_sync/run_streamed or via RunConfig.error_handlers
use_cases.precondition: Runner entry point called with error_handlers kwarg
use_cases.outcome: Handler stored in RunErrorHandlers TypedDict under 'max_turns' key

use_cases.name: Detect max turns exceeded
use_cases.description: When turn counter >= max_turns, RunLoop raises MaxTurnsExceeded('Max turns (N) exceeded')
use_cases.trigger: Turn count check at top of agent loop iteration
use_cases.outcome: MaxTurnsExceeded exception raised; run_data snapshot built via build_run_error_data()

use_cases.name: Dispatch custom max_turns handler
use_cases.description: resolve_run_error_handler_result() looks up 'max_turns' key in error_handlers dict, constructs RunErrorHandlerInput(error, context, run_data), calls handler (sync or async)
use_cases.precondition: error_handlers is not None and 'max_turns' key exists
use_cases.outcome: Handler callable invoked; result normalized to RunErrorHandlerResult

use_cases.name: Normalize handler return value
use_cases.description: Handler may return RunErrorHandlerResult | dict(final_output, include_in_history) | raw value | None. Dispatcher coerces all forms to RunErrorHandlerResult or None.
use_cases.validation: Extra dict keys beyond {final_output, include_in_history} raise UserError; dict missing final_output treated as raw value

use_cases.name: Return fallback final output
use_cases.description: If handler returns non-None RunErrorHandlerResult, run completes normally with handler's final_output instead of raising. include_in_history controls whether output is appended to conversation history.
use_cases.outcome: RunResult with handler-supplied final_output returned to caller

use_cases.name: Propagate MaxTurnsExceeded (no handler)
use_cases.description: If no error_handlers or 'max_turns' key absent, MaxTurnsExceeded propagates to caller. Exception carries run_data (RunErrorDetails) set by runner before re-raise.

use_cases.name: Raise InputGuardrailTripwireTriggered
use_cases.description: When input guardrail sets tripwire=True, raises InputGuardrailTripwireTriggered(guardrail_result). Caught in run_loop at multiple points (per-agent input check, streaming path). No custom handler supported — always propagates.
use_cases.outcome: InputGuardrailTripwireTriggered raised to Developer; guardrail_result field contains triggering InputGuardrailResult

use_cases.name: Raise OutputGuardrailTripwireTriggered
use_cases.description: When output guardrail sets tripwire=True after LLM response, raises OutputGuardrailTripwireTriggered(guardrail_result). Always propagates — no recovery hook.

use_cases.name: Raise ToolInputGuardrailTripwireTriggered
use_cases.description: When a tool-level input guardrail trips, raises ToolInputGuardrailTripwireTriggered(guardrail, output). No handler — propagates.

use_cases.name: Raise ModelBehaviorError
use_cases.description: Raised when LLM produces malformed JSON or calls a non-existent tool. No custom handler — propagates.

use_cases.name: Raise ToolTimeoutError
use_cases.description: Raised when a function tool exceeds its configured timeout and timeout_behavior='raise_exception'. No custom handler — propagates.

use_cases.name: Inspect run details on exception
use_cases.description: All AgentsException subclasses carry a run_data: RunErrorDetails | None attribute populated by the runner before propagating. Developer can inspect input, new_items, raw_responses, last_agent, guardrail_results.
use_cases.outcome: Developer retrieves partial run state for debugging/logging

key_classes.AgentsException: Base for all SDK exceptions; run_data: RunErrorDetails | None
key_classes.MaxTurnsExceeded: AgentsException; message: str; raised when turn count >= max_turns
key_classes.RunErrorHandlerInput: dataclass; error: MaxTurnsExceeded, context: RunContextWrapper, run_data: RunErrorData
key_classes.RunErrorHandlerResult: dataclass; final_output: Any, include_in_history: bool = True
key_classes.RunErrorData: dataclass; input, new_items, history, output, raw_responses, last_agent
key_classes.RunErrorDetails: dataclass; input, new_items, raw_responses, last_agent, context_wrapper, input_guardrail_results, output_guardrail_results

dispatch_flow: Dispatcher checks error_handlers.get('max_turns') → None → re-raise MaxTurnsExceeded
Dispatcher normalizes result: RunErrorHandlerResult | dict→RunErrorHandlerResult | raw→RunErrorHandlerResult | None→re-raise
Runner formats final_output via format_final_output_text() + validates via validate_handler_final_output()
Runner creates synthetic MessageOutputItem via create_message_output_item()
Runner returns RunResult with handler final_output
key_relationships: RunErrorHandlerResult produced by handler; consumed by Runner to build final RunResult
resolve_run_error_handler_result (error_handlers.py) is the sole dispatcher for max_turns handlers
All exception types inherit AgentsException; only MaxTurnsExceeded has handler dispatch
RunConfig.error_handlers (NotRequired) passes RunErrorHandlers to Runner entry points
Runner.run / run_sync / run_streamed all accept error_handlers kwarg; delegate to run_loop
important_notes: Only 'max_turns' error kind is currently supported for custom recovery. Guardrail tripwire exceptions have NO handler hook.
Handler return value is flexible: RunErrorHandlerResult, dict with 'final_output', or any raw value — all normalized to RunErrorHandlerResult.
include_in_history=False on RunErrorHandlerResult suppresses the fallback output from conversation history.
Handlers can be sync or async (MaybeAwaitable); dispatcher uses inspect.isawaitable() to await if needed.
RunErrorDetails (on exception) vs RunErrorData (passed to handler) are parallel but distinct: RunErrorDetails includes guardrail results; RunErrorData includes pre-computed history/output lists.
MaxTurnsExceeded is also raised in RunResultStreaming.stream_events() path (result.py line 759) for streaming runs.
